nodes.name: 鸟纲
nodes.summary: 鸟纲（Aves）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 沼泽
nodes.summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

nodes.name: 加岛绿鹭
nodes.summary: 加岛绿鹭（Butorides sundevalli）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如Lava heron。

nodes.name: 鹭科
nodes.summary: 鹭科（Ardeidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联57种鸟类，如白冠虎斑鷺、横纹虎鹭、船嘴鹭等57种鸟类。

nodes.name: 动物界
nodes.summary: 动物界（Animalia）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 脊索动物门
nodes.summary: 脊索动物门（Chordata）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 綠鷺屬
nodes.summary: 綠鷺屬（Butorides）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联4种鸟类，如Little heron、绿鹭、Lava heron等4种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鹈形目
nodes.summary: 鹈形目（Pelecaniformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联93种鸟类，如朱鹮、黑脸琵鹭、Plumbeous ibis等93种鸟类。

nodes.name: 加拉帕戈斯省
nodes.summary: 加拉帕戈斯省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Lava heron，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Lava heron
nodes.summary: The lava heron (Butorides sundevalli), also known as the Galápagos heron, is a species of heron endemic to the Galápagos Islands of Ecuador. It is considered by some authorities — including the American Ornithological Society and BirdLife International — to be a subspecies (or even just a colour morph) of the striated heron (B. striata), and was formerly "lumped" with this species and the green heron (B. virescens) as the green-backed heron. Description The adult is slate-grey to black, which allows it to blend in with the hardened lava. The back feathers typically have a silvery sheen and it has a short crest on its head. When breeding, the heron has a black beak and bright orange legs, but these fade to grey after the breeding season. Distribution and habitat These highly territorial birds are found in intertidal zones and mangrove swamps on all of the islands of Galápagos Province. Behavior Diet The lava heron stalks small crabs and fish slowly before quickly spearing and eating them. They have also been known to eat the flies that gather near cacti and occasionally smaller birds. Interactions These birds have little fear of humans. It has been noted they have flight behaviors, some of which may carry the purpose of territory defense/advertising. Calls Lava herons are typically seen hunched over and they have a sharp alarm call (described as a scow sound). During aggressive behavior they will use a skuk-skuk call. Breeding Unlike most herons, these birds nest in solitary pairs for one breeding season in either the lower branches of mangrove trees or under lava rocks. They can breed year-round, though typically from September to March, and can mate up to three times a year and have up to ten eggs each time. References Resources Heinzel, Hermann and Barnaby Hall. Galapagos Diary. Los Angeles; University of California Press, 2000.
